labels.infoText: The macronucleus is the larger of the two nuclei in paramecium. It controls the everyday functions and metabolism of the cell.
Cilia are short, hair-like structures covering the paramecium's surface. They beat in coordinated waves to propel the organism through water.
Contractile vacuoles collect excess water from the cell and expel it, maintaining osmotic balance in freshwater environments.
The micronucleus is smaller than the macronucleus and is involved in sexual reproduction and genetic recombination.
Food vacuoles are small sacs containing food particles being digested by enzymes.
